step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the assessment provided in the summary. The key point remains that the patient had a clear and characteristic presentation suggestive of an acute allergic reaction, possibly Stevens-Johnson Syndrome (SJS). Such reactions are known to present relatively acutely but also have a tendency to recur swiftly following re-exposure to the inciting allergens. The predicted time frame of 2 weeks does seem plausible because while some patients might experience a delayed return of symptoms, others could indeed have a much quicker resurgence of similar intensity rashes. Additionally, since the underlying mechanisms involve cellular immunity and inflammation processes that can reactivate within shorter intervals post-discontinuation followed by subsequent challenge, having a window around 2 weeks provides a balanced estimate reflecting possible variability across different individual responses yet maintaining alignment with common clinical outcomes observed in cases akin to this scenario.
